spec_match.rationale: The page now demonstrates continuous scroll timelines and the current named timeline-trigger plus animation-trigger pattern for discrete reveals.

interactive.rationale: A scroll-position range input, section jump buttons, and live readouts let visitors deliberately drive and inspect the scroll state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now echoes the feature index promise: scroll-position-based control of playing animations without relying on only an IntersectionObserver pattern.

edge_cases.rationale: Capability probes select CSS timeline-trigger, CSS scroll timeline, or JavaScript fallback paths and keep the page usable when native support is absent.

references.rationale: The see-also section includes the required ChromeStatus self-heal link and the CSS Animations Level 2 timeline-trigger spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: The page uses design-system variables for colors, pairs custom button backgrounds with text colors, and has a real control surface instead of a static card.

summary: The parallax concept now clears the stale critique: users can scrub or jump the scroll container, inspect active section and trigger path readouts, and see the current named trigger syntax with robust fallbacks and required references.
